perturbation.original_text: Shareholder will not, directly or indirectly, disparage Heritage and HBC, the business formerly conducted by Diablo, the business conducted by Heritage and HBC or any shareholder, director, officer, employee or agent of Heritage or HBC
perturbation.changed_text: Shareholder may, directly or indirectly, disparage Heritage and HBC, the business formerly conducted by Diablo, the business conducted by Heritage and HBC or any shareholder, director, officer, employee or agent of Heritage or HBC
perturbation.explanation: The original text imposes a strict prohibition against disparaging Heritage, HBC, or related parties. By changing 'will not' to 'may', the modified text permits disparagement. This directly contradicts the intent of a non-disparagement clause and creates uncertainty about the Shareholder's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Shareholder represents and warrants to Heritage and HBC that Shareholder has not executed any written agreement with any other person or entity that would prohibit Shareholder from entering into this Agreement. Further, Shareholder represents and warrants to Heritage and HBC that the execution of this Agreement by Shareholder will not conflict with any obligations or duties which Shareholder may have to prior employers or pursuant to any other agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited the shareholder from disparaging Heritage and HBC. The changed text now permits the shareholder to disparage Heritage and HBC. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text', where the shareholder represents and warrants that entering into the agreement will not conflict with any obligations or duties. Permitting disparagement creates a potential conflict with those obligations, particularly if those obligations involve upholding the reputation or interests of Heritage and HBC. This is a strong in-text contradiction based on the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition provided because permitting disparagement would likely be seen as a breach of contract, and thus introduces uncertainty about the Shareholder's obligations.

perturbation.original_text: The rights and remedies of the parties to this Agreement are cumulative and not alternative.
perturbation.changed_text: The rights and remedies of the parties to this Agreement are alternative and not cumulative.
perturbation.explanation: The original text specifies that the rights and remedies are cumulative, meaning parties can pursue multiple avenues of redress. The modified text makes them alternative, forcing parties to choose only one remedy. This fundamentally alters the enforcement options and creates conflict.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (c) Breach . Shareholder, Heritage and HBC each recognize and acknowledge that the Confidential Information and other knowledge Shareholder has about Diablo and has and will obtain from Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies is special and unique, and any violation of the covenants contained in this Agreement is likely to cause irreparable damage to Heritage, HBC or the Affiliated Companies. Therefore, the parties agree that, upon any breach of any covenant contained in this Section 3 by Shareholder, Heritage and HBC shall be entitled to an appropriate injunction for a violation of such covenant, threatened or actual, of such covenant, in addition to all other relief available under applicable law. If a court or arbitrator has determined that Shareholder has committed a breach by Shareholder of any covenant set forth in Section 3 of this Agreement, the Restricted Period will be extended by the period of the duration of such breach.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the rights and remedies are cumulative. The changed text states they are alternative. The contradicted text explicitly states that Heritage and HBC are entitled to 'an appropriate injunction...in addition to all other relief available under applicable law.' This "other relief" is incompatible with the 'alternative' remedies outlined in the changed text, but compatible with the 'cumulative' remedies in the original text, creating a strong contradiction. Therefore, the change introduces a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will be governed by the laws applied by courts of California to contracts entered into within that state by parties residing within that state and having no connection to any other state.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will be governed by the laws applied by courts of Delaware to contracts entered into within that state by parties residing within that state and having no connection to any other state.
perturbation.explanation: The original text designates California law as the governing law. The modified text changes this to Delaware law. This creates a legal conflict, as the contract's interpretation and enforcement will now be subject to a different legal framework, potentially leading to different outcomes in disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Whenever possible, each provision and term of this Agreement will be interpreted in a manner to be effective and valid, but if any provision or term of this Agreement is held to be prohibited or invalid, then such provision or term will be ineffective only to the extent of such prohibition or invalidity, without invalidating or affecting in any manner whatsoever the remainder of such provision or term or the remaining provisions or terms of this Agreement. If any of the covenants set forth in Section 3 of this Agreement are held to be unreasonable, arbitrary or against public policy, such covenants will be considered divisible with respect to scope, time and geographic area, and in such lesser scope, time and geographic area, will be effective, binding and enforceable against Shareholder to the fullest extent under California law.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies California law as the governing law for the agreement, and the contradicted text references California law when dealing with potentially unreasonable covenants. The changed text replaces 'California' with 'Delaware', meaning that the clause in the contradicted text specifying enforcement 'under California law' now directly conflicts with the governing law of the contract, which is now Delaware law. This creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement between the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement and supersedes all prior written and oral agreements and understandings between the parties with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement encompasses only some of the agreements between the parties regarding the main points of this Agreement; it does not overrule or replace any previous written or spoken agreements or understandings between the parties on these topics.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause clearly states that the current agreement is the only agreement between the parties and that it supersedes all other former agreements, either verbal or written. The changed version indicates that this isn't the only agreement between the parties and the parties may look to other agreements in understanding their arrangement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recitals . The recitals are incorporated herein and made a part of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that this agreement is the entire agreement between the parties, superseding all prior agreements. The changed text explicitly states that this is not the entire agreement and does not supersede prior agreements. The contradicted text incorporates the recitals into the agreement. If other agreements existed, incorporating recitals as part of *this* agreement becomes contradictory, because this agreement is supposed to be the 'entire agreement,' according to the original text.
